other_observers: Alas, none--I had to leave the site to place a call to Peter Gent (this was 1987 after all). He came out but all he was was a Tundra Swan which I'm sure was a different bird.;
specific_location: On the south side of the "Ricky Weiser" bridge crossing Boulder Creek about 0.5 miles east of 75th Street.
description: This was a small bird bigger than a kinglet/goldfinch or most warblers/vireos; about the size of a housefinch. It had a fairly long and pointy black fill generally brownish gray on the back with the tail being darker. The chest was white but streaked with difuse gray/brown streaks and the entire belly and vent was a bright, "house finch" pink.Behaviors: perched and not very active in a small bushCall: NAPlumage: adult
